name: Anirvan
core_thesis: Article-backed thinker stub created during primitives_v2 Phase 2 from `data/articles/source/samkhya-anirban.md`.

cross_engagements.brief: Abhinavagupta presses the same substrate structure axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `one-self-standing` rather than `many-coordinate-reals`.
Adi Sankara presses the same manifestation status axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `sublatable-not-null` rather than `real-transformation`.
Anandabodha presses the same selfhood structure axis in the phenomenological register, but with the value `witness-self` rather than `substantial-self`.
Alfred North Whitehead presses the same temporal mode axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `process-primary` rather than `substance-primary`.
Akalanka presses the same practice-path axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `meditative-discipline` rather than `knowledge-discipline`.
Anantakrishna Sastri presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `identity-with-ground` rather than `isolation-or-discriminative-release`.
Baladeva Vidyabhushana presses the same causation model axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `unchanged-ground-with-changing-power` rather than `real-transformation`.

engaged_works.title: Veda Mīmāṃsā
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored modern monograph; bibliographic ascription is uncontroversial.
engaged_works.summary: Anirvan's three-volume Bengali commentary on the Ṛgveda, in the tradition of Sri Aurobindo's *Secret of the Veda* but pushing the symbolic-psychological reading of Vedic ritual further. The work treats the Vedic *yajña* as an interior process of consciousness — the *agni* as the ascending will of aspiration, *soma* as the descending grace of inspiration — and reads the *sūktas* as the records of integral spiritual-psychological discoveries. The three volumes cover successive layers of the Ṛgvedic corpus; Volume 1 supplies the methodological introduction and treats the *agni-sūktas*; volumes 2 and 3 extend the reading across the *indra-* and *soma-* corpora. The work is the principal post-Aurobindonian Bengali continuation of the symbolic-Vedic reading and is one of the few twentieth-century Bengali-language Vedānta-Sāṃkhya hybrid texts of major scholarly weight.

engaged_works.title: Letters from a Baul: Life within Life
engaged_works.title_iast: Letters from a Baul: Life within Life
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored modern monograph; bibliographic ascription is uncontroversial.
engaged_works.summary: An English-language compilation of Anirvan's correspondence on the inner life, the discipline of awareness, and the relation between formal Vedānta-Sāṃkhya doctrine and the lived spiritual journey. The Baul-tradition title gestures toward the Bengali wandering-mystic lineage that Anirvan (Pranab-darshan in some renderings) felt close to. The work supplies the experiential-pedagogical complement to the doctrinal *Veda Mīmāṃsā* — letters to disciples on the working of *prāṇa*, the discipline of attention, the integration of life within the inner life. The work is read alongside Aurobindo's *Letters on Yoga* in the comparative corpus as one of the principal twentieth-century Indian epistolary records of integral spiritual practice.
